key_insight: Automated analysis of collagen architecture in prostate biopsy images identified extracellular matrix features that can help predict which localized prostate cancer patients are more likely to recur after radiotherapy.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this image-based ECM signature could support post-radiotherapy risk stratification alongside standard pathology and Gleason grading.
method: 378 quantitative ECM features were extracted from picrosirius red-stained diagnostic prostate biopsies.
A matched 1:1 retrospective case-control cohort of 422 CHHiP trial patients was analyzed.
Machine learning and Shapley interpretation were used to link ECM patterns with tumor vs normal tissue, Gleason grade group, and recurrence.
evidence_signal: In the 422-patient matched cohort, the model found recurrence-associated ECM patterns that were also enriched in Gleason 4+3 versus 3+4 disease.
watch_item: This is a retrospective analysis from a trial cohort, so external validation and real-world reproducibility are needed before clinical use.
paper_title: Identification of collagen features predictive of recurrence following radiotherapy for localised prostate cancer: a retrospective case control analysis
